```

- Actual value: result is [[0, 0], [1, 0], [0, 1]] (all three original cells survive since each has 2 neighbors, but [1,1] is never added because the implementation only filters existing live cells and does not create new ones)
```

Let me verify: each cell in the L-shape `[[0,0], [1,0], [0,1]]` has exactly 2 live neighbors, so they all survive with the current implementation. But `[1,1]` (dead, with 3 live neighbors) is NOT in the result because the implementation lacks reproduction logic. This matches the prediction.

The test failed with: `expected [ [ 0, 0 ], [ 1, 0 ], [ 0, 1 ] ] to deep equally contain [ 1, 1 ]`

This is exactly what was predicted: the implementation returns only surviving live cells and never births new cells, so `[1,1]` is missing from the result.
